steps.title: Find the lower bound
steps.content:
For every real value of $x$, the exponential part is positive.

$$2^x>0$$

So the numerator is always greater than $5$.

$$6(2^x)+5>5$$

steps.title: Divide by the coefficient
steps.content:
Dividing by $17$ gives the range condition for $g(x)$.

$$g(x)>\frac{5}{17}$$

steps.title: Choose the greatest bound
steps.content: As $x$ becomes very negative, $2^x$ gets closer to $0$, so $g(x)$ gets closer to $\frac{5}{17}$ from above but never equals it. Therefore the greatest possible value of $k$ is $\frac{5}{17}$. The answer is C.
